title: GOING SHOPPING
conversation: Amy is at a clothing store shopping for a new pair of pants. Lisa, a saleswoman, asks her if she needs any help. Lisa: May I help you? Amy: I'm just browsing. Lisa: Let me know if I can help. My name is Lisa. Amy: Do these pants come in black? Lisa: Yes, but we're sold out. They're so popular, we can't keep them in stock. Amy: Do you have any black pants on sale? Lisa: Yes, these are marked down from $69.95 to $24.95. Amy: That's a good buy, but I'm not crazy about the fabric. Lisa: What about these pants? These are on sale for $49. Amy: That's a little pricey, but I'll try them on. (Amy enters the fitting room with the pants. A few minutes later, Lisa comes to check on her.) Lisa: How are the pants? Amy: A little snug. Lisa: That style does run small. Let me bring you the next size up. (Lisa hands Amy the pants and Amy puts them on.) Amy: These pants are a little big, aren't they? Lisa: No, they fit nicely! Amy: They're baggy in the back, don't you think? Lisa: No, they're perfect. Take my word for it. Amy: I'm just not sure. I think I'd better hold off for now.

idioms.meaning_en: Looking at goods in a store without the immediate intention of buying anything.
idioms.meaning_vi: chỉ đang xem qua (không có ý định mua ngay)
idioms.example: The clerk asked if I needed help, but I told her I was just browsing.

idioms.meaning_en: To be produced or available in a specific color, size, or style.
idioms.meaning_vi: có sẵn (theo màu sắc, kích cỡ, kiểu dáng)
idioms.example: Does this dress come in a smaller size?
idioms.context: Do these pants come in black?

idioms.meaning_en: Having all of a particular item sold; no longer available.
idioms.example: I wanted to buy the new iPhone, but the store was completely sold out.

idioms.meaning_en: Available for sale and present in the store.
idioms.example: We don't have any more of these shoes in stock right now.
idioms.context: They're so popular, we can't keep them in stock.

idioms.meaning_en: Offered at a lower price than usual.
idioms.example: I bought these jeans while they were on sale for 50% off.
idioms.context: Do you have any black pants on sale?

idioms.example: All winter coats have been marked down to make room for spring clothes.
idioms.context: Yes, these are marked down from $69.95 to $24.95.

idioms.meaning_en: Something that is worth the price; a bargain.
idioms.example: At only $10, that shirt is definitely a good buy.
idioms.context: That's a good buy, but I'm not crazy about the fabric.

idioms.meaning_en: To not like or enjoy something very much.
idioms.example: I'm not crazy about spicy food, so let's eat somewhere else.
idioms.context: That's a good buy, but I'm not crazy about the fabric.

idioms.example: The food was delicious, but the restaurant was a little pricey.
idioms.context: That's a little pricey, but I'll try them on.

idioms.meaning_en: To put on a piece of clothing to see if it fits or looks good.
idioms.example: You should try them on before you buy them.
idioms.context: That's a little pricey, but I'll try them on.

idioms.example: These shoes are a little snug; do you have a larger size?

idioms.meaning_en: To be smaller in size than is typical for that item of clothing.
idioms.meaning_vi: cỡ hơi nhỏ (so với tiêu chuẩn thông thường)
idioms.example: Italian designer clothes often run small.

idioms.meaning_en: To believe someone's statement without requiring further proof.
idioms.example: It's a great movie, just take my word for it.
idioms.context: No, they're perfect. Take my word for it.

idioms.meaning_en: To delay or wait before doing something.
idioms.example: We should hold off on buying a new car until next year.
idioms.context: I think I'd better hold off for now.
